title: Ray County Commissioner's Meeting 4/29
showNotes:
# County Commission Meeting Summary

## Meeting Overview
The county commission conducted a regular meeting addressing multiple infrastructure projects, public concerns, administrative matters, and contract decisions. The session included public comments, engineering project updates, and executive actions.

## Public Comment Period

### Road and Drainage Issues
Several citizens raised concerns about road maintenance and drainage problems. One resident expressed frustration about ongoing ditch maintenance issues on their road, noting that work had been performed on the opposite side of the road from where it was needed. The resident announced the launch of a website, f3oads.com (f is for fix), designed to allow county residents to report road issues. The site will route complaints to appropriate special road districts and county email addresses, with an open map displaying all submitted issues.

Another resident at 1434 Aikenwood in Raymoor reported significant drainage problems causing rock to wash away and plug pipes on their property. The resident, who retired from road work after approximately 30 years, suggested that recent ditch work had accelerated water flow rather than slowing it down, exacerbating erosion issues. The resident offered to purchase a new pipe if the county would install it. Commission members acknowledged the concern and indicated they would work toward a resolution.

### Sunshine Request Policy
A citizen inquired about changes to sunshine request fees and whether the old rate would be honored for requests submitted prior to the fee change. The commission confirmed the old rate would be respected. The citizen was advised to resubmit any unfulfilled requests, particularly those related to votes from closed sessions that are required to be made public within 72 hours. The commission noted some requests were pending legal review. When asked about prepaying for multiple requests, the commission indicated requests must be paid individually due to accounting requirements.

### Road Complaint Form
Residents presented a Google Doc form they had prepared for the county website to standardize road complaint submissions. However, commissioners expressed preference for a county-initiated and controlled form rather than one created by private citizens, indicating they would develop their own official documentation process.

## Administrative Actions

### Minutes and Payables
The commission voted to untable and approve minutes from the 22nd and 23rd, with motions passing unanimously.

### Planning and Zoning - Tucker Razors Plat
One commissioner recused themselves from a plat approval for Tucker Razors due to a conflict of interest. The remaining commissioners approved the plat by motion.

## Engineering Projects Update

### Consultant Selection Process
The county's engineering firm provided a detailed explanation of qualification-based selection (QBS) requirements under the federal Brooks Act and Missouri's mini-Brooks Act. Key points included:
- Federal funds require qualification-based selection of engineers
- For contracts under $200,000 in engineering fees, the county can evaluate consultants from MoDOT's approved list
- For contracts over $200,000, a formal Request for Qualifications process is required
- Existing relationships with qualified firms can be continued without a new RFQ process

### Myers Road Bridge
The engineering team reported progress on the Myers Road bridge design:
- A vertical curve has been incorporated into the bridge design, reducing the elevation increase from 6-8 feet to approximately 5 feet
- The substructure will consist of cast-in-place walls with pile footings extending 20-25 feet to reach solid rock, due to unstable glacial till conditions
- Temporary construction easements will likely be needed from two to four property owners
- Final plans are expected to be submitted in June or July
- Construction bidding would follow, with work beginning next spring

### West 216th Bridge
Discussion focused on replacing this structure with precast boxes:
- Design includes two 12-foot lanes with headwall supports at inlet and outlet
- The county will need to hire a contractor for installation, as county forces lack the necessary equipment
- County forces can handle ancillary work such as backfilling
- The structure falls within the existing 60-foot right-of-way corridor
- Formal easements may not be necessary; property owner permission letters may suffice

### West 84th Street Bridge
Engineers presented updated findings showing the need for a larger structure than originally anticipated:
- Survey revealed approximately 18-foot depth, requiring a 78.5-foot structure
- Proposed design uses precast NU girders
- Construction estimates range from $500,000 to $600,000, with engineering costs of $130,000
- Recent comparable bids in another county ranged from approximately $410,000 to $682,000
- The commission retains the option to reject all bids and pursue value engineering if costs come in too high

### Lexington/Henrietta Bridge Demolition
The commission discussed demolition options for this structure:
- MoDOT allows demolition to count toward BRzO (Bridge Replacement Off-System) requirements
- The county may receive soft match credit for demolition costs
- Two options presented: removing only the superstructure (beams and deck) or full removal including substructure
- Partial removal would significantly reduce costs; substructure appears stable and could remain indefinitely
- Full removal near railroad tracks requires extensive safety measures, increasing expenses
- No immediate decision was made on scope

### Bridge Applications
The commission needs to identify priority bridges for the next round of BRO applications:
- Applications expected to open April 1st
- The engineering firm will provide the project list when released
- Bridges are rated 1-10 based on inspection results; structures rated 4 or below (poor condition) qualify for the list

## Bid Opening Action - Holt College
Via phone, the commission addressed an irregularity from a recent bid opening where a single bid was received one hour late:
- The county attorney advised the wording allows discretion given only one bidder
- Motion to waive the irregularity and review the bid for consideration passed unanimously

## Announcements and Future Agenda Items
- Child abuse awareness recognition was held at 11:30, with participants wearing blue
- Museum roof replacement was completed and praised; the previous roof was approximately 45 years old
- Right-of-way questions from residents were directed to the planning and zoning office for review
- Next week's agenda will include the Eagleton Agreement with rent changes
shortSummary:
This County Commission meeting addressed several infrastructure and administrative issues. Public comments focused on road maintenance and drainage problems, with citizens proposing a centralized online form for reporting complaints. The commission also clarified Sunshine Law procedures, confirming that new fee schedules would apply and that citizens should resubmit any pending requests.

A significant portion of the meeting involved updates from Great River Engineering on bridge projects. They detailed design progress for the Myers Road bridge, presented cost estimates for a larger structure on West 84th Street, and recommended federal funding applications for two additional bridges. In formal action, the commission approved a motion to waive irregularities and consider a late bid for the Holt College project, approved meeting minutes, and signed a planning and zoning document before moving into a closed session.
